title: Working Near Building Envelope at High Elevation
description: Reviewed hazards associated with curtain wall installation at floors 38-42. Covered 100% tie-off requirements. Demonstrated proper anchor use on curtain wall track. Reviewed wind hold procedures - operations suspended above 25 mph. Covered heat illness prevention in south Florida sun.
notes: All curtain wall crew and surrounding floor workers attended. Updated wind speed log procedure. Distributed SPF 50 sunscreen to all outdoor workers - heat illness prevention. All signed attendance sheet.

title: Stone and Marble Handling Safety
description: Reviewed proper rigging for stone slabs. Covered use of marble lifter clamps versus slings. Demonstrated correct vacuum pad usage for large format tile. Reviewed dropped object hazards. Covered manual handling and back injury prevention for heavy stone pieces. Required 2-person lift for any piece over 50 lbs.
notes: All marble masonry and tile crews attended. Updated stone rigging procedure posted at marble staging area. Vacuum pads inspected - 2 replaced due to wear. Mandatory marble lifter clamp use confirmed for all crane picks.

title: Hospitality Finish Work PPE Requirements
description: Covered PPE requirements for interior finish phase. Reviewed chemical hazards from adhesives sealants and coatings in enclosed guest rooms. Covered proper respirator selection for VOC exposure. Reviewed SDS requirements for all finish products. Discussed ventilation requirements during painting and coating.
notes: All drywall electrical plumbing and finish crews attended. SDS binder updated and placed at each floor landing. 12 workers required N95 respirators - ordered and distributed same day. Ventilation fan deployment plan distributed to all floor foremen.

title: Electrical Safety - Guest Room Trim Phase
description: Covered NFPA 70E requirements for trim-out phase. Reviewed energization sequence for floors 8-10 - live circuits now present. Covered lockout-tagout procedures for trim work near energized panels. Reviewed GFCI requirements in bathrooms and wet areas. Covered arc flash hazards.
notes: All electricians and MEP trim crews attended. LOTO stations stocked at each electrical room. GFCI outlet test schedule distributed. All live panels labeled with arc flash stickers. Workers confirmed understanding of floor energization status.
